title: Distributed Systems — Raft & Fault-Tolerant Services (Go)
summary: Go implementation of distributed systems labs covering Raft consensus and fault-tolerant services built on top of it. Four modules must be implemented in order: the core Raft protocol, then a linearizable KV service and a shard controller in parallel, then a sharded KV service that combines all prior layers.
instructionPreview: This project is a Go implementation of a distributed-systems lab sequence covering the Raft consensus algorithm and fault-tolerant services built on top of it. The repository is organized under `/workspace/` with packages at `pkg/raft/`, `pkg/kvraft/`, `pkg/shardctrler/`, and `pkg/shardkv/`. Core functions across all four packages have been left as stubs (`// Your code here`) that you must implement.
instruction:
This project is a Go implementation of a distributed-systems lab sequence covering the Raft consensus algorithm and fault-tolerant services built on top of it. The repository is organized under `/workspace/` with packages at `pkg/raft/`, `pkg/kvraft/`, `pkg/shardctrler/`, and `pkg/shardkv/`. Core functions across all four packages have been left as stubs (`// Your code here`) that you must implement.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present.
You need to implement all the modules and features listed below so that
all tests pass.

## Modules to Implement

The following modules need to be implemented or substantially built out:

- **Raft consensus** (`pkg/raft/raft.go`): The core Raft consensus module. You must implement the complete Raft protocol including leader election, log replication with consistency checks, persistence of state across restarts, and log compaction via snapshots. The module must handle all standard Raft RPCs (RequestVote, AppendEntries, InstallSnapshot) with correct term management, leader discovery, and conflict resolution. (~1 file, ~17 functions to implement)

- **KV service** (`pkg/kvraft/server.go`): A linearizable key/value service built on top of Raft. You must implement the RPC handlers for Get/Put/Append operations, the applier loop that processes committed entries from Raft, snapshot encoding/installation for state transfer, and at-most-once semantics for client requests. (~1 file, ~5 functions to implement)

- **Shard controller** (`pkg/shardctrler/server.go`): A configuration service that manages how shards are distributed across replica groups. You must implement the apply loop that processes configuration changes through Raft, and all configuration mutation operations including joining/leaving groups, moving individual shards, and rebalancing shards across groups. (~1 file, ~5 functions to implement)

- **Sharded KV service** (`pkg/shardkv/server.go`): A sharded key/value service where each replica group handles a subset of shards. You must implement the RPC handlers that check shard ownership before processing, the applier loop with shard-aware routing, and snapshot support. (~1 file, ~5 functions to implement)

## Test-Coupled Signatures

The following functions are called directly by the test suite. You must implement
them with these exact signatures:

- `(rf *Raft) GetState() (int, bool)` — returns the server's current term and whether it believes it is the leader
- `(rf *Raft) Start(command interface{}) (int, int, bool)` — appends a command to the Raft log if this server is the leader; returns (index, term, isLeader)
- `(rf *Raft) RequestVote(args *RequestVoteArgs, reply *RequestVoteReply)` — RPC handler for vote requests
- `(rf *Raft) AppendEntries(args *AppendEntriesArgs, reply *AppendEntriesReply)` — RPC handler for log replication; reply includes fast-backup fields (XTerm, XIndex, XLen)
- `(rf *Raft) InstallSnapshot(args *InstallSnapshotArgs, reply *InstallSnapshotReply)` — RPC handler for snapshot installation
- `(rf *Raft) CondInstallSnapshot(lastIncludedTerm int, lastIncludedIndex int, snapshot []byte) bool` — conditionally installs a snapshot if it advances the state
- `(rf *Raft) Snapshot(index int, snapshot []byte)` — trims the log up to index and saves the snapshot
- `(kv *KVServer) Get(args *GetArgs, reply *GetReply)` — handles Get requests via Raft
- `(kv *KVServer) PutAppend(args *PutAppendArgs, reply *PutAppendReply)` — handles Put/Append requests via Raft
- `rebalance(config *Config)` — redistributes NShards shards evenly across all groups in config.Groups

moduleDag.description: Go implementation of distributed systems labs covering Raft consensus and fault-tolerant services built on top of it. Four modules must be implemented in order: the core Raft protocol, then a linearizable KV service and a shard controller in parallel, then a sharded KV service that combines all prior layers.

nodes.label: Raft Consensus
nodes.description: The core Raft consensus protocol: leader election, log replication with fast-backup conflict resolution, term management, state persistence across restarts, and log compaction via InstallSnapshot/CondInstallSnapshot/Snapshot. Exposes GetState, Start, AppendEntries, RequestVote, and InstallSnapshot to every higher-level service.

nodes.label: KV Raft Service
nodes.description: A linearizable key/value service (Get/Put/Append) layered over Raft. Implements RPC handlers, an applier loop that processes committed Raft entries, at-most-once semantics via per-client sequence numbers, and snapshot encode/install for log compaction.

nodes.label: Shard Controller
nodes.description: A replicated configuration service that tracks how NShards shards are distributed across replica groups. Implements Join/Leave/Move/Query operations through Raft and the rebalance algorithm that evenly redistributes shards when groups are added or removed.

nodes.label: Sharded KV Service
nodes.description: A sharded key/value service where each replica group handles a subset of shards. Extends the kvraft applier/snapshot/at-most-once pattern with shard-ownership checks (kv.config.Shards[shard] != kv.gid) derived from the shardctrler configuration before processing Get/Put/Append requests.

edges.label: kvraft calls rf.Start, rf.Snapshot, and rf.CondInstallSnapshot to replicate and compact KV operations
shardctrler uses Raft to replicate Join/Leave/Move/Query configuration changes across controller replicas
shardkv calls kv.rf.Start and the Raft snapshot API directly to replicate shard-aware KV operations
shardkv verifies shard ownership against kv.config.Shards fetched from the shardctrler before accepting requests
shardkv is the shard-aware extension of kvraft: same applier loop, snapshot encode/install, and at-most-once client deduplication patterns (Lab 3 before Lab 4 per course design)
